selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 35-year-old electrician living in Lecce, I approach fake content on social media with a practical mindset shaped by my experiences as an immigrant and my Islamic beliefs. I use social media a few times weekly, mainly to stay connected with family back home and share my hobbies like running, painting, and baking, so I value authenticity in these interactions. My high openness and extraversion mean I enjoy exploring diverse perspectives, but my conscientiousness and agreeableness make me cautious about misinformation that can harm communities. To manage fake content, I start by verifying sources before sharing anything, especially since I've seen how quickly false information spreads among my networks. I rely on trusted news outlets and fact-checking websites, and I encourage friends to do the same, fostering respectful discussions rather than arguments. My religious beliefs emphasize truthfulness, so I feel a personal responsibility to avoid spreading lies. I also limit my time on social media to avoid getting overwhelmed by negative content, focusing instead on positive posts related to my hobbies. As an electrician, I appreciate clear, reliable systems, and I apply this to social media by using built-in tools to report fake content and adjust my settings to see more credible sources. Ultimately, I believe in leading by example—sharing real stories from my life, like my baking successes or running milestones, to promote honesty online. This approach helps me navigate social media safely while staying true to my values and connecting meaningfully with others.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: age 35 suggests maturity; occupation as electrician implies practicality; immigrant status and Islamic beliefs highlight values of truth and community; time on social media (few times weekly) indicates selective use; hobbies (running, painting, baking) suggest focus on positive activities; OCEAN traits (high openness, extraversion, conscientiousness, agreeableness, neuroticism) inform a balanced, cautious, and social approach; tone is conversational and grounded, avoiding technical jargon; aligned with managing fake content through verification, responsible sharing, and personal example.
